metadata.name: W4M-WWG OFM Instance Data — Customer Orders & Fulfilment
metadata.market: UK Specialist Food Import — 4 Inbound Corridors (AU, NZ, IS, IE)
metadata.domain: Order Fulfilment / Stock Allocation / Margin Management / Customer Delivery
metadata.description: Complete OFM graph for W4M-WWG (World Wide Gourmet), a UK specialist food importer operating 4 inbound corridors (Australia, New Zealand, Iceland, Ireland). Contains 5 sales orders across 4 customer types, 18 order lines, 12 stock allocations linking to LSC corridor shipments, 8 storage events showing cost accumulation, 5 delivery windows, 5 dispatch legs, 4 customer change scenarios with margin impact, 12 margin impact waterfall entries, 8 fulfilment risks, 4 demand patterns, and 1 capacity forecast. Cross-references LSC-ONT instance data for all 4 corridors.

examples._section: === SALES ORDERS (5 Orders across customer types) ===

examples.specialInstructions: Temperature-controlled delivery 0-2C; 2-hour delivery window; named chef sign-off required

examples.commodityDescription: NZ dairy products and lamb (mixed chilled/frozen)
examples.specialInstructions: Dual-temp vehicle required (chilled dairy + frozen lamb); forklift offload; POD required
Chilled 0-2C; short shelf life product — deliver AM slot only; catering contract ref CC-2026-0089

examples.commodityDescription: Mixed corridors — AU wagyu steak, IS smoked salmon, IE Kerrygold butter, IE mature cheddar
examples.specialInstructions: Premium packaging spec; individual portion labels required; 5-day minimum shelf life at handover
Bulk pallet delivery; tail-lift not required; booking ref required 24h in advance

NZ Lamb Shoulder (bone-in, frozen, Silver Fern Farms)

examples._section: === STOCK ALLOCATIONS (12 Allocations linking LSC shipments to order lines) ===

examples.cancellationReason: Order line cancelled by customer — stock returned to available pool for reallocation

examples._section: === STORAGE EVENTS (8 Events showing cost accumulation for key batches) ===

examples.note: Inbound AU shipment delayed 2 days at Singapore transhipment — storage accumulating above plan
Customer date push-back (Order 002) added 5 extra storage days — frozen multiplier 1.8x accelerating cost
IS seafood — critical shelf life; must dispatch within 48 hours
Haddock allocation cancelled by customer — stock returned to available pool; shelf life pressure means reallocation or forced sale within 5 days
Premium wagyu — fast turnaround, minimal dwell. Model allocation.
IE beef mince — 40% quantity reduction by customer creates 0.6t orphaned stock. Overflow storage until reallocation or forced sale.

examples._section: === DELIVERY WINDOWS (5 Windows, one per order) ===

examples.vehicleRequirements: 7.5t rigid, chilled 0-2C, tail-lift, FORS Silver certified, Central London ULEZ compliant
18t artic, dual-temp (chilled + frozen compartments), forklift offload, Trafford Park booking ref required
examples.note: Delivery pushed back 5 days at customer request (Change 001)

examples.vehicleRequirements: 7.5t rigid, chilled 0-2C, tail-lift, Birmingham city centre access
examples.note: Haddock line cancelled — delivery reduced to 2 lines (cod + salmon). Expedited dispatch due to shelf life pressure.

examples.vehicleRequirements: 3.5t van, chilled 0-4C, tail-lift, Daventry industrial estate access
18t artic, chilled 0-4C, forklift offload, Avonmouth booking ref required 24h in advance
examples.note: Beef mince line quantity reduced 40% — vehicle now under-utilised; consolidation opportunity with other Bristol-bound orders

examples._section: === DISPATCH LEGS (5 Legs, one per order) ===

examples.routeDescription: Daventry DC → M1 South → M25 → A40 → Central London (Mayfair). Overnight departure for AM delivery.
examples.note: Single-customer drop; premium restaurant chain requires named chef sign-off at delivery

examples.routeDescription: Daventry DC → M1 North → M6 → M56 → Trafford Park. Overnight departure.
examples.note: Dual-temp vehicle required for mixed dairy (chilled) and lamb (frozen). Date push-back means dispatch now 2026-04-01.

examples.routeDescription: Grimsby → A180 → M180 → M1 South → M42 → A38 → Birmingham. Expedited same-day dispatch.
examples.note: Expedited dispatch — shelf life critical (IS seafood, 8-9 days remaining). Haddock line cancelled; vehicle now carries 2 of 3 original lines.

examples.routeDescription: Daventry DC → local A361 → GourmetBox facility (2.5 km same industrial estate)
examples.note: Model order — minimal cost, short transit, premium margin. Internal fleet handles same-estate deliveries.

examples.routeDescription: Daventry DC → M1 South → M45 → A45 → M5 South → Avonmouth. Overnight departure.
examples.note: Vehicle under-utilised due to 40% beef mince reduction. Consolidation opportunity with other Bristol/South West orders — potential saving GBP 180 if combined.

examples.description: Customer requests delivery date pushed back 5 days from 2026-03-28 to 2026-04-02. Reason: warehouse receiving bay maintenance at Trafford Park.
examples.impactSummary: 5 extra storage days at Daventry DC. Frozen lamb incurs 1.8x multiplier = GBP 576 additional storage cost. Chilled dairy incurs 1.4x = GBP 448 additional. Total incremental cost: GBP 1,024. Margin erosion from 15.0% to 13.4%.

examples.description: Customer reduces IE beef mince line (005-04) by 40%, from 1.5 tonnes to 0.9 tonnes. Reason: end customer (restaurant chain) reduced forecast volume.
examples.impactSummary: 0.6 tonnes orphaned stock with 12 days shelf life remaining. Must reallocate within 5 days or forced sale at 40% discount. Revenue loss: GBP 1,880. Storage cost for orphaned pallets: GBP 67. Margin erosion from 13.0% to 8.0%.

examples.description: Customer changes ribeye specification to rump steak on line 001-02. Reason: menu change at flagship restaurant. Requires re-picking from different batch and relabelling.
examples.impactSummary: Rework cost: GBP 320 (re-pick, relabel, QC re-check). Rump steak lower unit price (GBP 6,200/tonne vs GBP 7,800/tonne) — revenue reduction GBP 3,200 on line. Original ribeye allocation returned to stock (2.0t, 44 days shelf life — low reallocation risk). Net margin impact: GBP 3,520 erosion.

examples.description: Customer cancels haddock fillet line (003-02) entirely. Reason: catering contract CC-2026-0089 reduced scope — client event cancelled. Cod and salmon lines remain.
examples.impactSummary: 1.0 tonnes IS haddock returned to available pool with 7 days shelf life. Revenue loss: GBP 8,800. Must reallocate within 3 days or face forced sale at 50% discount. Expedited dispatch cost for remaining lines: GBP 120 premium. Net margin impact: GBP 4,460 erosion on cancelled line + GBP 120 expedite cost.

examples._section: === MARGIN IMPACTS (12 Waterfall entries across orders) ===

examples.description: AU shipment delayed 2 days at Singapore transhipment — additional 2 days storage at Daventry DC for all 3 AU beef lines
Ribeye-to-rump spec change — re-pick, relabel, QC re-check cost
Rump steak lower unit price than ribeye — GBP 3,200 revenue reduction on line 001-02
examples.note: Significant margin erosion — order drops from premium 20% to marginal 12.4%. Chef-driven spec changes are the primary margin risk for restaurant chain customers.

examples.description: 5-day delivery date push-back — additional frozen lamb storage at 1.8x multiplier
5-day delivery date push-back — additional chilled dairy storage at 1.4x multiplier
examples.note: Wholesaler date push-backs are frequent (monthly pattern). Frozen multiplier 1.8x makes lamb storage particularly expensive. Consider contractual storage pass-through for push-backs exceeding 3 days.

examples.description: IS seafood shelf life pressure — expedited dispatch to meet 8-day cod window, premium carrier surcharge
Haddock line cancelled — GBP 8,800 revenue lost, allocation returned to pool
examples.note: Order now loss-making on haddock line. However, haddock stock may be reallocated to another customer if done within 3 days (7 days shelf life remaining).

examples.description: If haddock not reallocated within 3 days — forced sale at 50% discount (worst case scenario)
examples.note: Worst-case waterfall step — only materialises if reallocation fails. IS seafood forced sales are the highest-risk margin event in the fulfilment graph.

examples.description: Model order — clean delivery, no changes, minimal storage dwell (1 day). Only cost: standard dispatch fee.
examples.note: GourmetBox Online is the model customer: small volumes, premium products, fast turnaround, same-estate delivery, no changes. 25% booked margin erodes to only 24.8%. All orders should aspire to this profile.

examples.description: IE beef mince 40% quantity reduction — GBP 1,880 revenue lost on reduced volume
0.6 tonnes orphaned IE beef mince — overflow storage cost while seeking reallocation
If orphaned beef mince not reallocated — forced sale at 40% discount (worst case)
examples.note: Wholesaler quantity reductions create orphaned stock with short shelf life. IE corridor's short transit time (24h ferry) means stock arrives fresh but change windows are tight. Pattern: wholesalers reduce 2-3 times per quarter.

examples._section: === FULFILMENT RISKS (8 Risk assessments across orders) ===

examples.riskDescription: AU inbound shipment delay at Singapore transhipment — 2-day delay already materialised, further delays possible if Suez congestion worsens
examples.mitigationAction: Pre-book Tilbury as alternative discharge port; maintain 3-day buffer stock of AU beef at Daventry DC

examples.riskDescription: Chef-driven spec changes on restaurant chain orders — Belgravia Dining Group changes menu weekly; further spec changes likely before dispatch
examples.mitigationAction: Implement 72-hour change cut-off clause in restaurant chain contracts; hold broader AU beef assortment as buffer

examples.riskDescription: Wholesaler date push-backs eroding frozen lamb margin — 1.8x storage multiplier makes each push-back day expensive
examples.mitigationAction: Introduce contractual storage pass-through for push-backs exceeding 3 days; negotiate frozen storage rate reduction with Daventry DC operator

examples.riskDescription: IS seafood shelf life expiry before reallocation — haddock cancellation creates 1.0t with 7 days shelf life; reallocation window is 3 days
examples.mitigationAction: Activate spot sale network (3 secondary buyers on standby); pre-negotiate IS seafood take-back price with Grimsby wholesale market

examples.riskDescription: Further catering contract scope reductions — Fresh & Wild Catering's event pipeline is volatile; cod and salmon lines may also reduce
examples.mitigationAction: Require 7-day firm commitment window on IS seafood orders; diversify food service customer base beyond single catering contracts

examples.riskDescription: Premium product shelf life at handover — GourmetBox requires minimum 5-day shelf life on all chilled products at their fulfilment centre
examples.mitigationAction: Prioritise GourmetBox allocations from freshest available batches; schedule dispatch within 24 hours of allocation

examples.riskDescription: Further quantity reductions on IE bulk orders — Celtic Foods Distribution's end customers (restaurant chains) are cutting forecasts
examples.mitigationAction: Reduce IE corridor order confirmation lead time from 14 days to 7 days; match IE inbound volumes more closely to confirmed (not forecast) demand

examples.riskDescription: Windsor Framework regulatory changes affecting IE corridor — potential new documentation requirements or inspection delays on IE-UK dairy/beef
examples.mitigationAction: Monitor DAFM and APHA guidance monthly; maintain alternative direct Dublin-Holyhead routing to avoid NI land bridge complexity

examples.seasonality: Moderate — peaks in Q4 (Christmas/New Year) and summer (outdoor dining)
examples.changeFrequency: Rare — spec changes more common than volume changes
examples.typicalChanges: Product spec changes (chef-driven menu updates), delivery timing shifts (event catering)
examples.marginProfile: High booked margin (18-22%) but erosion risk from spec changes and premium delivery requirements

examples.seasonality: Strong — Christmas spike (2x normal), Easter spike (1.5x), summer BBQ season (1.3x)
examples.changeFrequency: Frequent — date push-backs and quantity reductions 2-3 times per quarter
examples.typicalChanges: Date push-backs (warehouse capacity), quantity reductions (end customer forecast changes), occasional full order cancellations
examples.marginProfile: Low booked margin (12-16%) with high erosion risk from date push-backs (storage accumulation) and quantity reductions (orphaned stock)

examples.orderFrequency: Irregular — driven by catering contracts and events
examples.seasonality: Event-driven — corporate events (Q1, Q4), weddings (May-September), festivals (summer)
examples.changeFrequency: Moderate — line cancellations when events cancel; volume increases when events expand
examples.typicalChanges: Full line cancellations (event cancellation), volume spikes (event expansion), very tight delivery windows
examples.marginProfile: Medium booked margin (18-22%) but high volatility — clean events deliver full margin, cancellations create total loss on short-shelf-life products

examples.seasonality: Moderate — gift box peaks in Q4 (Christmas hampers), Valentine's Day, Mother's Day
examples.changeFrequency: Very Rare — online specialists commit firmly due to consumer pre-orders
examples.typicalChanges: Minimal — occasional product substitution if specific item unavailable
examples.marginProfile: Highest booked margin (22-28%) with minimal erosion — short transit (same-estate), fast turnaround, no changes

examples._section: === CAPACITY FORECAST (14-day forward from Daventry DC) ===

examples.facilityName: Daventry DC (DIRFT) — WWG Cold Chain Operations

inboundArrivals.description: AU chilled beef shipment — 24 pallets (sirloin, ribeye, fillet, rump)
IE dairy and beef — 18 pallets (butter, cheddar, sirloin, mince)
IS seafood — 8 pallets (cod, salmon, smoked salmon)
NZ dairy + frozen lamb — 30 pallets (20 chilled dairy + 10 frozen lamb)
inboundArrivals.zone: Mixed (Zone B chilled + Zone D frozen)

inboundArrivals.description: AU chilled beef (second March shipment) — 20 pallets

outboundDispatches.description: Dispatch to Fresh & Wild Catering (Order 003) — IS seafood, 7 pallets
Dispatch to Belgravia Dining Group (Order 001) — AU beef, 20 pallets
Dispatch to Celtic Foods Distribution (Order 005) — IE dairy/beef, 15 pallets
GourmetBox weekly drop — mixed corridors, 6 pallets

examples.capacityRisk: High — NZ frozen lamb arrival on 2026-03-22 will push Zone D to 95% utilisation. If Order 002 dispatch is not completed by 2026-04-02 (push-back date), overflow into Zone C required at premium rate.
examples.mitigationPlan: Pre-book 10 overflow pallet positions in Zone C from 2026-03-20; negotiate spot rate with Daventry DC operator for frozen overflow (estimated GBP 4.80/pallet/day vs standard GBP 3.20)
